rulings.Case Title: LESLIE HILL ET AL VS. CRANE CO.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 28, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. The three page excerpt of Leslie Hill's September 6, 2018 deposition is insufficient to satisfy defendant's burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' punitive damages claim. The Court did not consider the unsigned declaration of Craig Mountz submitted by defendant in support of the motion because the declaration did not comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 2015.5. The Court also did not consider defendant's notice of errata because it was untimely served under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2). Further, the Court did not consider defendant's reply for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court under Local Rule 2.7(B). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.= (503/CML)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 28, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Nancy Williams, Brayton Purcell's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Margarita Branch is granted. Brayton Purcell provided proper notice to Ms. Branch pursuant to CRC 3.1362 and the Court has received no opposition. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell notifies Ms. Branch of this tentative ruling and she states that she does not wish to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
